name: Donostia - Sugar Mommy
rawSourceText: Set in Donostia, a costal, sophisticated city in the north coast of Spain. Contains famous restaurants and art expositions. There's a strong contrast between the Donostia of the wealthy, with their class dynamics, and working-class neighborhoods. Prices of housing have risen steadily, which forces plenty of young people to move out. Many of the wealthy and famous aspire to live in a villa in the hillside or hilltop with views overlooking the Donostia bay.

facts.fact: Donostia is a coastal city located on the north coast of Spain.
Donostia has a bay that is visible from elevated hillside and hilltop positions.
Donostia is surrounded by hills, some of which are dotted with villas and upscale residences.
Donostia is renowned for its sophisticated culinary scene, including famous high-end restaurants.
Donostia hosts notable art expositions that attract a cultured, affluent audience.
Donostia is sharply divided between a wealthy elite class and working-class neighborhoods, with stark contrasts in lifestyle and resources.
The wealthy class in Donostia maintains rigid internal class dynamics and social hierarchies.
Housing prices in Donostia have risen steadily over time, making the city increasingly unaffordable.
Rising housing costs force many young people to leave Donostia and relocate elsewhere.
Among the wealthy and famous of Donostia, owning a hilltop or hillside villa with bay views is a coveted status symbol and aspiration.
Hilltop and hillside villas in Donostia offer panoramic views overlooking the Donostia bay.
Donostia's sophistication and prestige attract wealthy residents, celebrities, and the socially ambitious.
Working-class residents of Donostia are economically marginalized relative to the city's wealthy elite, particularly as housing costs climb.
The illicit and secretive mixing across class boundaries in Donostia's intimate social spaces — restaurants, art expositions, private villas — carries a charged, transgressive quality.
